title: pithecellobium bubalinum
intro: Pithecellobium bubalinum, also known as the Cow Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. The Pithecellobium bubalinum plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of the plant include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Pithecellobium bubalinum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be obtained by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or alcohol.
